title: Data Visualisation
text:
Data Visualisation
  Data Visualisation
  
    Selected visualisation projects, I did for fun, for class, or for freelance work. The Guardian Australia featured one of my TidyTuesday visualisations on Scottish Munros in their biweekly data-viz best-of a while ago.

title: Software
text:
Software
  Software
  
    Open-source code accompanying research papers, side projects, and tools. 
    Most work is in R and Python; some JavaScript.
  




  
    
      R
      disturbanceBorealLPJ
      Reproduction code for the boreal forest disturbance and climate change paper (Biogeosciences, 2025).
    
  

  
    
      R
      stableEWS_paper
      Reproduction of the early warning signs paper — tipping points with non-Gaussian-stable noise (Scientific Reports, 2025).
    
  

  
    
      Jupyter / Python
      alphastableTransient
      Code for "Disturbances and long transients create illusion of stable states".
    
  


  
    
      R
      borealRecovery
      Analysis code for post-disturbance boreal forest recovery modelling.
    
  

  
    
      JavaScript
      backgammon
      A simple interactive backgammon board in the browser.

title: Research
text:
Science
  Research
  
    My research explores how ecosystems recover after disturbances — wildfires, armed conflict, 
    and land-use change — under shifting climates. I combine dynamic vegetation modelling, 
    remote sensing, and statistical approaches to understand resilience and inform 
    climate adaptation strategies.
  


Publications


  
    2025
    Early warning signs for tipping points in systems with non-Gaussian-stable noise.
    Scientific Reports ·
    doi
  

  
    2025
    Disentangling future effects of climate change and forest disturbance on vegetation composition and land surface properties of the boreal forest.
    Biogeosciences ·
    doi
  

  
    2024
    Frost matters: Incorporating late-spring frost in a dynamic vegetation model regulates regional productivity dynamics in European beech forests.
    Biogeosciences ·
    doi
  

  
    2022
    Quantifying the impacts of land cover change on gross primary productivity globally.
    Scientific Reports ·
    doi
  

  
    2021
    The potential of direct steam cracker electrification and carbon capture & utilization via oxidative coupling of methane as decarbonization strategies for the chemical industry.
    Applied Energy ·
    doi

title: Lucia S. Layritz
text:
Lucia S. Layritz
  

Hi, I am Lucia and I am a climate scientist currently based at UC Berkeley.

I am broadly interested in modelling the impact of extreme events on the coupled land surface - atmosphere system, especially the impact of wildfire and otehr ecosystem disturbances onf vegetation, and the impact of climate change on ecosystem regeneration.

My research employs complex Global Dynamic Vegetation Models as well as low- dimensional stochastic dynamical systems, where I am particularly interested in heavy-tailed data and model uncertainty across scales.  

My work aims to inform strategies for climate adaptation and ecological resilience, and I work closely with land managers and ecologist in U.S. National Parks across the Western US.



Current Position
Postdoctoral Researcher, Eric & Wendy Schmidt Center for Data Science and Environment, UC Berkeley. Supporting the National Park Service Climate Change Decision Support project.


Education

PhD Life Science Technical University of Munich
MSc Mathematical Modelling King’s College London, UK
MSc Environmental Science and Engineering Technical University of Berlin, Germany Research stays: Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research · American University of Beirut

title: Communication
text:
Communication
  Communication
  
    I write, edit, and sketch about my science to a variety of audiences. I particularly care about communicating an intuition for climate science and modeling without using a lot of maths. 
  


Book on Klimamodellierung verstehen (Understanding Climate Modeling)
I co-wrote and illustrated a popular science text book on climate modeling, to be published in German by Springer Nature in Summer of 2026. The book is intended for a general audience, and covers the basics of climate modeling, how models work, and what they can and cannot do. Fingers crossed for an English edition in the future!



Editor-in-Chief — EGU Biogeosciences Blog
Since 2024, I am serving as the Editor-in-Chief for the European Geosciences Union’s Biogeosciences Division blog. I lead a team of four topical editors to maintain the blog as a space for and by the Biogeosciences community. If you’re interested in writing for the blog, please reach out to me at lucialayr@berkeley.edu.



Public Writing
The existential modelling crisis – and how to overcome it A reflection of modeling as a scientific practice, and how approach it as a PhD Student. GeoLogs, 2025. https://blogs.egu.eu/geolog/2025/09/05/the-existential-modelling-crisis-and-how-to-overcome-it/
“Was unterm Strich zählt” (What counts in the end)
politische ökologie/ political ecology, issue 02/2020
A lifecycle analysis of plastic emissions — examining what the numbers actually mean for policy and production.
